prompt: It isn't true that some detectives are slow
hint: Identify the subject and predicate. Are they individuals (lowercase) or classes (uppercase)?
explanation: "It isn't true that some detectives are slow" denies that even one detective is slow — so NO detective is slow. That is the universal-negative (E) form: no D is S. Both the subject (detectives) and predicate (slow) are classes, so both are uppercase. (¬∃x(Dx ∧ Sx) is equivalent to ∀x(Dx → ¬Sx).)
